about.noDescription: No description yet — the project lead hasn’t written one.

stepsSection.headingNeedsHelp: {total, plural, one {# step} other {# steps}}. {needing, plural, one {# needs help} other {# need help}} right now.
stepsSection.headingProgress: {total, plural, one {# step} other {# steps}}. making progress right now.
stepsSection.emptyDescription: Once the project lead breaks the work down into steps, they’ll show up here so you can pick one to claim.

join.memberNote: You’re a member. {title} shows in your dashboard and you can see members-only updates.
join.leaveConfirm:
{count, plural, one {You have # step assigned to you on this project. If you leave, it goes back to the team.} other {You have # steps assigned to you on this project. If you leave, they go back to the team.}}

Are you sure you want to leave?
join.memberCardBody: This project is in your dashboard. Claim a step to start contributing, and check the Updates tab for news.
join.requestSentBody: The project lead will get a notification. You’ll become a member once they accept.
join.joinCardBody: Join the project to claim steps, see updates in your dashboard, and chat with the team.

stats.title: Project numbers
stats.noContributors: No contributors yet — be the first.
stats.signInToSee: <link>Sign in</link> to see who’s working on this.

details.title: Details
details.daysAgo: · {count, plural, one {# day ago} other {# days ago}}

updates.publicUpdateCount: {count, plural, one {# public update} other {# public updates}}.
updates.hiddenGate: {count} members-only {count, plural, one {update is} other {updates are}} hidden.
updates.toSeeThem: {count, plural, one {to see it.} other {to see them.}}
updates.emptyLead: Post the first update above — members get notified, and public updates show visitors that this project is active.
updates.emptyViewer: When the project lead posts updates or steps get completed, they’ll show up here.
updates.visibleToMembers: Visible only to the {count, plural, one {# project member} other {# project members}}
updates.visibleToAnyone: Visible to anyone browsing the project
updates.confirmDelete: Delete this update? This can’t be undone.

milestones.peopleJoined: {count, plural, one {# person joined —} other {# people joined —}}

chooser.heading: Start a project.
chooser.intro: You can begin with an empty plan, or fork a blueprint — a proven plan from someone who’s done this before.
chooser.scratchDescription: An empty plan. Best when your project is unusual, very local, or you’ve already worked out the steps yourself.
chooser.blueprintDescription: Copy freely. Pre-built plans for repair cafés, pocket forests, mutual aid groups and more — browse the library, then fork one and adapt it to your community.

statusCard.title: What's happening, in one word?
statusCard.desc: This appears at the top of the project page, in listings, and in everyone’s dashboard. Set it honestly — "Needs help" reaches more people than "In progress" when you really need them.

descriptions.defining: Still working out the plan. Not ready for contributors yet — people can follow along, but there’s nothing to do.
descriptions.needs_help: You need more people. Pushes the project to the top of skill matches and the home page.
descriptions.in_progress: Work is happening and the team is mostly set. The normal state for most live projects.
descriptions.completed: The work is done. Moves to the “Finished” tab. You can reopen the project at any time.

basics.title: What is it?
basics.desc: Two sentences are enough. You can add more detail later.
basics.descriptionHelp: The first paragraph is what shows up on the project card.
basics.descriptionPlaceholder: What are you trying to make happen, and why does it matter?

cover.title: A picture for your project.
cover.desc: Used on the project page and the cards on Browse and My projects. Optional — a colourful gradient stands in if you skip it.
cover.hint: Wide image works best — at least 1200×600. PNG, JPG, WebP or GIF, up to 8 MB.

location.title: Location & access.
location.desc: Helps the right people find you. You’ll only show the area, never an exact address.
location.countryHelp: Shown on the project card and used by the browse-page country filter.
location.addressHelp: Shown to people who join. Skip this if your meet-up spot changes or you’d rather not publish it.
location.addressPlaceholder: e.g. The Old Library, 2 Wallis Road, E9 5LH
location.languageHelp: Used by the browse-page language filter.

remote.label: Can people contribute remotely?
remote.someHint: If “some steps only”, you can mark each step as remote-okay later.

open.label: Open to the world
open.description: Anyone can join instantly and start contributing.

approval_required.label: Approval needed
approval_required.description: Joins land in your inbox as a request to accept or decline.

org.label: Organisation
org.note: Organisation projects show the organisation’s name and count towards its contribution totals.

public.label: Public
public.description: Anyone can find and join it — with the organisation’s name on it.

org_members.label: Members only
org_members.description: Only members of the organisation can see or join it.

steps.title: Steps to make it real.
steps.createDesc: Break the work into small pieces someone could do in an evening. You can always add more later — but a starter list of 3–8 helps people see how to help.
steps.editDesc: Add, edit, reorder, or remove steps. If you delete a step someone has claimed, that person is removed from it — be careful.

saveBar.savedVariant: Saved as a blueprint variant — it now shows under the family.
saveBar.savedNew: Saved as a blueprint — others can fork it now.
saveBar.savedAsBlueprint: Saved as blueprint — others can fork it now.

adminDelete.confirmBody: You're about to permanently delete {name}. This can't be undone.
adminDelete.projectConsequence: All its steps, contributions and logged time go with it.
adminDelete.blueprintConsequence: Its steps are removed; projects forked from it and any variants are kept (just unlinked).
